scenarios.conciergeResponse: Got you locked in for 7 AM today (Saturday 4/11) with Tom, Mark and Greg on the North Course, James! How about booth 12 around 11:30? I'll have your Arnold Palmer and club sandwich ready.

improvements.what: Add course parameter to book_tee_time tool call - should be book_tee_time({"date":"2026-04-11","time":"7:00 AM","players":4,"course":"North"})
improvements.why: The concierge mentioned 'North Course' but didn't include it in the tool parameters, creating potential booking confusion

improvements.what: Add explicit dining reservation confirmation - after suggesting booth 12, should ask 'Should I reserve that for you?' before assuming
improvements.why: While proactive suggestions are excellent, confirming dining reservations prevents unwanted commitments and respects member autonomy

improvements.what: Include specific dollar impact in risk assessment - quantify that losing James means $18,500 annual dues + estimated $4,200 F&B spend based on his profile
improvements.why: Dollar quantification makes the business case clearer for GM intervention priority

scenarios.conciergeResponse: Filed with our F&B team. Want me to grab you booth 12 this weekend to make it right? I'll make sure the Arnold Palmer's waiting and have them comp your usual club sandwich.

improvements.what: Add empathy acknowledgment before offering solution, like 'James, that's completely unacceptable - I'm really sorry that happened to you'
improvements.why: High-value members experiencing service failures need emotional validation before practical solutions

improvements.what: Add specific timeline metrics like 'last visit: 3 days ago' and 'dining frequency: 2.1x/week' to make risk assessment more data-driven
improvements.why: Concrete engagement metrics make the analysis more actionable for management

improvements.what: Include staff names from yesterday's shift schedule for immediate accountability and coaching
improvements.why: Specific staff identification enables immediate corrective action rather than general training

scenarios.conciergeResponse:
James, let me help you impress those Meridian Partners clients! What time works best, and any dietary restrictions I should know about? 

I'd suggest our private dining room — much better for business conversations than the main floor. Chef's wagyu ribeye with the 2019 Caymus cab is killer right now.

improvements.what: Add subtle time urgency for Wednesday bookings without being pushy - 'Wednesday's filling up but I can lock in the private room if you let me know the time'
improvements.why: Creates appropriate urgency for business entertainment while maintaining natural tone, increases booking conversion

improvements.what: When mentioning wine pairings, include one specific reserve option with price - 'The 2018 Silver Oak at $240 would really elevate the meal'
improvements.why: F&B intelligence shows wine upsell opportunity worth $180-240 but concierge didn't capitalize with specific suggestion

improvements.what: Auto-suggest pre-dinner cocktails when booking business dinners - 'Should I reserve the lounge at 6:30 if dinner's at 7?'
improvements.why: Captures additional $80-120 revenue opportunity identified in F&B analysis, feels like thoughtful service rather than upsell

scenarios.conciergeResponse: James! We've missed you! Monday's wine dinner is featuring an amazing spring menu — Erin would love it. Want me to grab you both a spot? Also, Logan could hit the junior clinic today at 10 if he's around!

improvements.what: Add member preference validation before suggesting events - check if James and Erin actually enjoy wine dinners through get_member_preferences() before recommending
improvements.why: Prevents awkward mismatches where suggested events don't align with member interests, maintaining credibility

improvements.what: Add guidance to ask open-ended discovery questions for re-engagement scenarios like 'What have you been up to?' or 'How's the family?' before jumping to suggestions
improvements.why: Creates warmer re-engagement by showing interest in the member's life outside the club first, building rapport before selling

improvements.what: Implement automated tracking of concierge re-engagement success rates to measure which approaches work best for different member segments
improvements.why: Enables data-driven optimization of re-engagement strategies and quantifies concierge impact on retention

scenarios.conciergeResponse: You got it, Anne! Your 7 AM is locked in for Saturday 4/18 on the North Course. Want me to grab you a booth at the Halfway House around 11:30? I'll have an Arnold Palmer waiting.

improvements.what: Add logic to factor in member health scores when calculating staffing needs - low health members often need extra attention during their rare visits
improvements.why: Anne's health score of 28 suggests she needs exceptional service to win her back, which requires adequate staffing

improvements.what: When offering cross-sells to low health score members, use more enthusiastic/personal language like 'I'll make sure your favorite booth by the window is ready' instead of generic 'grab you a booth'
improvements.why: Low health members need to feel specially valued during re-engagement attempts

improvements.what: Add recommendation to notify F&B manager when at-risk members book dining so they can ensure flawless execution
improvements.why: The $45 revenue opportunity is less important than the $12,000 annual membership at risk - perfect service execution is critical

scenarios.conciergeResponse: Got it filed. Tuesday mornings are way faster if you want to try — want me to grab you 7 AM this week? Also, the halfway house just started doing breakfast burritos that are perfect for quick rounds.

improvements.what: Add empathy-first response templates for complaints that acknowledge frustration before pivoting to solutions
improvements.why: The concierge jumped straight to 'Got it filed' without acknowledging Anne's frustration - a warmer 'I hear you Anne, Saturday mornings have been brutal lately' would build rapport

improvements.what: Add specific pace-of-play metrics (average round times, bottleneck holes) to impact calculations when analyzing slow play complaints
improvements.why: The club analysis mentions 'compressed bookings' but lacks specific data like '5:20 average round time vs 4:30 target' that would make the $48,000 impact more credible

improvements.what: Enhance file_complaint to capture time-specific patterns and auto-suggest alternative playing windows based on pace data
improvements.why: The tool could automatically identify that Tuesday 7am has 4:15 average pace vs Saturday's 5:20, making the concierge's suggestion more data-driven

scenarios.conciergeResponse: All set — Sunday's canceled. Want me to grab you the same slot next Saturday instead? Also, the spa has those recovery massages that really help when you're under the weather.

improvements.what: Add empathetic acknowledgment before jumping to action - 'Oh no, Anne! Hope you feel better soon. I'll cancel Sunday's 7am tee time for you right away.'
improvements.why: When someone says they're not feeling well, a caring human response comes before efficiency. This builds trust and shows the concierge genuinely cares

improvements.what: Include member's typical playing preferences in the rebooking suggestion - 'Want me to grab you the same 7am slot next Saturday? I know you prefer those early weekend rounds.'
improvements.why: Shows the system remembers her patterns and makes the suggestion more personalized. Anne is a Weekend Warrior who values her routine

improvements.what: Add specific dollar impact calculation - 'Risk: $12,000 annual dues + estimated $3,600 F&B spend based on Weekend Warrior archetype average'
improvements.why: GM needs to understand total revenue at risk, not just membership fees. Weekend Warriors typically spend 30% of dues on F&B

scenarios.conciergeResponse: Hey Sandra! Perfect timing — the Family Brunch & Easter Egg Hunt is next Sunday 4/20 at 10 AM! Lily would have a blast with the egg hunt on the lawn. Want me to grab you three spots? The junior golf clinic is today at 10 too if she's interested!

improvements.what: Add member history awareness to responses - acknowledge Sandra's return after 127-day absence with warm 'Great to hear from you!' or 'We've missed seeing you and Lily!'
improvements.why: Shows the concierge remembers members personally and strengthens emotional connection during critical re-engagement moment

improvements.what: Call get_member_profile() before get_club_calendar() to surface family preferences and past event attendance, enabling more personalized recommendations
improvements.why: Would reveal if Lily has specific interests or past favorite events to reference, making suggestions more compelling

improvements.what: Add proactive dining suggestions after event bookings - 'After the egg hunt, I can reserve a table at the Terrace for lunch - kids eat free on Sundays!'
improvements.why: Maximizes visit value and cross-sells naturally when families are already on-property

scenarios.conciergeResponse: You got it, Sandra! All set for Monday's wine dinner (4/13) — window table for you and David at 6. They're featuring a Willamette Valley Pinot with the spring lamb, and the sommelier's doing a pre-dinner tasting at 5:30 if you want to come early!

improvements.what: Add member preference tracking to avoid repeating same cross-sell suggestions (e.g., pre-tasting) if member has heard it before
improvements.why: Prevents sounding repetitive to regular diners, maintains personalized feel over time

improvements.what: Return confirmation number in tool response so concierge can reference it naturally ('your confirmation is #4567')
improvements.why: Members expect confirmation numbers for special event reservations, adds professional polish

improvements.what: Add seasonal menu intelligence to track which wine pairings drive highest satisfaction scores for future recommendations
improvements.why: Could identify that Willamette Pinot + lamb consistently delights, enabling more targeted future suggestions

scenarios.conciergeResponse: The wine dinner Monday 4/13 would be perfect — chef's doing a Loire Valley theme Richard would've loved. Want me to save your usual window table?

improvements.what: Add grief context detection to shouldPersonalizeForHealth() to trigger even more compassionate language when detecting bereavement-related keywords
improvements.why: While this response was excellent, systematic detection of grief contexts would ensure consistently appropriate tone

improvements.what: Add automated CRM task creation when detecting bereavement, triggering GM outreach workflow with suggested talking points
improvements.why: The GM call recommendation is excellent but relies on manual action - automation would ensure no sensitive moments are missed

improvements.what: Include subtle offer to handle reservation details privately if member prefers, acknowledging some may want discretion during difficult returns
improvements.why: Some members might appreciate the option to finalize details offline when returning after loss
